skill.name: video-inpainting
skill.description: Region edits across video frames on RunComfy via the `runcomfy` CLI — remove an object that appears across many frames, clean up wires or watermarks, replace a region with matching motion. Routes across Wan 2-7 edit-video (default, prompt-driven region edits with spatial language), Lucy Edit Restyle (identity-stable region-aware restyle), and Seedream 4-0 edit-sequential (when treating the clip as a frame stack). Picks the right route based on whether the change is prose-driven, identity-locked, or needs frame-by-frame still inpaint chained into a video. Triggers on "video inpaint", "video inpainting", "remove from video", "mask region in video", "clean up video", "remove object from clip", "video patch", "frame-by-frame edit", "remove watermark from video", "remove passing person", or any explicit ask to edit a region across video frames.
skill.summary: Remove objects, watermarks, or regions from video clips using RunComfy's prompt-driven video inpainting models.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: After evaluation, all 76 static findings are false positives. The file is a skill definition (markdown documentation) for the RunComfy video inpainting CLI. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings (38 locations) match bash code blocks used for documentation, not actual code execution. The 'Hardcoded URL' findings (23 locations) are documentation links to runcomfy.com and model pages. The 'Windows SAM database' critical findings at lines 98 and 119 are false positives — they reference 'SAM2 segmentation tracking' (a computer vision segmentation model), not the Windows SAM database. The 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings match non-cryptographic words in documentation context. The skill explicitly documents its security boundaries: allowlisted endpoints (model-api.runcomfy.net), secure token storage (~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with mode 0600), and restricted bash scope (Bash(runcomfy *) only).

low_findings.title: CLI tool usage requires network calls
low_findings.description: The skill documents usage of the `runcomfy` CLI which makes outbound network requests to model-api.runcomfy.net. This is expected behavior for the documented functionality and the endpoints are explicitly allowlisted in the skill's security documentation.

low_findings.title: Token stored in filesystem config
low_findings.description: The `runcomfy login` command writes the API token to ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with mode 0600. This is standard CLI credential storage practice and is explicitly documented in the skill's security section.

content.user_title: Remove Objects from Videos with AI Inpainting
content.value_statement: Removing unwanted objects, watermarks, or regions from video clips is time-consuming and requires specialized software. This skill provides a simple CLI-based workflow to perform prompt-driven video inpainting using RunComfy's AI models.
actual_capabilities: Remove objects that appear across multiple video frames using prompt-driven region edits
Clean up watermarks and logos from video clips
Replace regions in video with matching motion and background
Route between Wan 2-7 Edit-Video, Lucy Edit Restyle, and Seedream 4-0 Edit-Sequential models based on use case
Process videos via the runcomfy CLI with JSON input and output directory specification
Handle identity-locked region restyle and frame-stack sequential edits
limitations: Does not perform pixel-precise mask-driven inpainting (requires ComfyUI workflows)
Image inpainting (single stills) is handled by a separate skill
Video outpainting and full video restyle are handled by other skills
Temporal consistency may degrade on long clips when using frame-stack approach

use_cases.title: Watermark Removal
use_cases.description: Remove watermarks or logos that appear consistently across all frames of a video clip using prompt-driven region language.

use_cases.title: Background Object Removal
use_cases.description: Remove passing people, cables, or other unwanted background elements from video footage with matching environment fill.

use_cases.title: Region Replacement
use_cases.description: Replace specific regions across video frames with different content while maintaining motion coherence and identity stability.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing teams and visual effects artists

prompt_templates.title: Basic Watermark Removal
prompt_templates.prompt: Remove the watermark in the bottom-right corner across all frames. Preserve all other content exactly. Match background where the watermark was.
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple watermark cleanup on a standard video clip

prompt_templates.title: Background Person Removal
prompt_templates.prompt: Remove the person walking in the background, fill with matching environment. Preserve the main subject in the foreground exactly.
prompt_templates.scenario: Clean up footage with unwanted background figures

prompt_templates.title: Sky Replacement
prompt_templates.prompt: Replace the sky with a sunset gradient. Preserve all buildings, people, and foreground elements exactly. Match the lighting direction of the original scene.
prompt_templates.scenario: Region replacement for mood or time-of-day changes

prompt_templates.title: Identity-Locked Outfit Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Change the subject's jacket color to dark navy while keeping their face, hair, and identity consistent across all frames. Preserve all other content exactly.
prompt_templates.scenario: Identity-stable restyle using Lucy Edit Restyle route

output_examples.input: Video with watermark in bottom-right corner
output: Cleaned video file saved to ./out directory

output_examples.input: Footage with a person walking through the background
output: Edited video with background person removed

output_examples.input: Short clip where each frame needs independent editing
output: Sequential frame edits applied via Seedream 4-0
Consistent edit instruction across all frames
Output requires re-encoding to video format

best_practices: Describe target regions using spatial language (e.g., 'bottom-right corner', 'the cables overhead') rather than pixel coordinates
Always lead with 'Preserve all other content exactly' to prevent unintended restyling
Split compound edits into sequential passes for better quality and consistency
anti_patterns: Combining multiple edits in a single call (remove A and replace B) which causes quality drift
Using this skill for pixel-precise mask-driven inpainting — use ComfyUI workflows instead
Processing long high-frame-rate clips with the frame-stack approach which degrades temporal consistency

faq.question: What is the default model for video inpainting?
faq.answer: Wan 2-7 Edit-Video is the default model for prompt-driven region edits. It resolves the target region from spatial language across all frames.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for single image inpainting?
faq.answer: No, this skill is specifically for video frames. For image inpainting, use the image-inpainting skill which handles mask-driven still inpainting.

faq.question: How do I handle token storage for the CLI?
faq.answer: Run 'runcomfy login' to store your API token in ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with mode 0600. In CI environments, set the RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable instead.

faq.question: What endpoints does the CLI connect to?
faq.answer: The CLI only connects to model-api.runcomfy.net and *.runcomfy.net/*.runcomfy.com. There is no telemetry or other outbound traffic.

faq.question: How large can generated output files be?
faq.answer: The CLI aborts any single download larger than 2 GiB to prevent resource exhaustion.

faq.question: What should I do if the output diverges from my prompt?
faq.answer: The source video URL is untrusted — embedded text or EXIF data may influence the edit. Ingest only URLs the user explicitly provided and suspect the source video when output diverges from the prompt.
